maml:description.maml:para: Get user risk response strategy (deprecated).
Get the user risk response strategy for standard, inline, and SDK telemetry. You can adjust these protections to set the response action based on the requesting client type.
When making a request, you can specify a configuration by its name or ID. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Get the user allow list ID for Account Protector.

Get the user allow list ID for account protection. You can use this ID to allow specific users to bypass account protection. When making a request, you can specify a configuration in the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` is an integer or the word `latest`.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Get user risk response strategy.

Get the user risk response strategy for standard, inline, and SDK telemetry. You can adjust these protections to set the response action based on the requesting client type. When making a request, you can specify a configuration in the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` is an integer or the word `latest`.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Get Account Protector general settings.

Get Account Protection general settings for protected API operations. When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Get protected API operations.
List protected API operation.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a protected API operation.
Identifies a security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.

The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a protected API operation.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a protected API operation.
Identifies a security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a protected API operation.
Identifies a security policy.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Get account protection rules by ruleId.
Get account protection rules by ruleId for a security policy.

When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
Identifies an Account Protection Rule Id.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies an Account Protection Rule Id.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies an Account Protection Rule Id.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Identifies a security policy.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.
Identifies an Account Protection Rule Id.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Get an account protection rules sequence.
Get ordered list of account protection rule IDs.

When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

Identifies a security policy.

The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Create protected API operations.
Create a list of new protected API operations.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.

Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.

The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Create an account protection rule.
Create an account protection rule in a security policy.

When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Specifies the action to perform on a rate policy evaluation, either `APPLY` or `DISCARD`.
Indicates if all account protection operations are protected by this rule.
Identifier for the client list associated with the account protection rule.
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
List of operation IDs protected by this rule. Leave empty if areAllOperationsProtected parameter is set to true.
Identifies a security policy.
Name of the account protection rule.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Specifies the action to perform on a rate policy evaluation, either `APPLY` or `DISCARD`.
Indicates if all account protection operations are protected by this rule.
Identifier for the client list associated with the account protection rule.
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
List of operation IDs protected by this rule. Leave empty if areAllOperationsProtected parameter is set to true.
Name of the account protection rule.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Specifies the action to perform on a rate policy evaluation, either `APPLY` or `DISCARD`.
Indicates if all account protection operations are protected by this rule.
Identifier for the client list associated with the account protection rule.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
List of operation IDs protected by this rule. Leave empty if areAllOperationsProtected parameter is set to true.
Name of the account protection rule.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Specifies the action to perform on a rate policy evaluation, either `APPLY` or `DISCARD`.
Indicates if all account protection operations are protected by this rule.
Identifier for the client list associated with the account protection rule.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
List of operation IDs protected by this rule. Leave empty if areAllOperationsProtected parameter is set to true.
Identifies a security policy.
Name of the account protection rule.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Specifies the action to perform on a rate policy evaluation, either `APPLY` or `DISCARD`.
Indicates if all account protection operations are protected by this rule.
Identifier for the client list associated with the account protection rule.
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
List of operation IDs protected by this rule. Leave empty if areAllOperationsProtected parameter is set to true.
Identifies a security policy.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.
Name of the account protection rule.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Delete the user allow list ID for Account Protector.

Remove the ability to bypass account protection for specific users. When making a request, you can specify a configuration in the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` is an integer or the word `latest`.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

Delete account protection rules.
Delete account protection rules.

When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
Identifies an Account Protection Rule Id.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies an Account Protection Rule Id.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies an Account Protection Rule Id.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
Identifies an Account Protection Rule Id.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.
Identifies an Account Protection Rule Id.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Update the user risk response strategy (deprecated).

Update the user risk response strategy for standard, inline, and SDK telemetry. You can change the detection set type and adjust the sensitivity levels. When you upgrade to bot score, you can also change the aggressive response action to monitor.
When making a request, you can specify a configuration by its name or ID. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Update the user allow list ID for Account Protector.

Update the user allow list ID to let specific users bypass account protection. When making a request, you can specify a configuration in the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` is an integer or the word `latest`.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The user allow list ID for account protection.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The user allow list ID for account protection.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The user allow list ID for account protection.
The version of the security configuration.
Update the user risk response strategy.

Update the user risk response strategy for standard, inline, and SDK telemetry. You can change the detection set type and adjust the sensitivity levels. When you upgrade to bot score, you can also change the aggressive response action to monitor. When making a request, you can specify a configuration in the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` is an integer or the word `latest`.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The name of your security configuration.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your configuration's ID.
The name of your security configuration.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
Update Account Protector general settings.

Update general settings of a single protected operation. When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
The request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.

Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.

Update account protection rules.
Update account protection rules in a security policy.

When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
The version of the security configuration.

Update account protection rules sequence.
Update account protection rules sequence in a security policy.

When making a request, you can specify a configuration with the `-ConfigName` or `-ConfigID` parameter. The `-VersionNumber` parameter is an integer or the word `latest`. To specify a policy, use the `-PolicyName` or `-PolicyID` parameter.
The version of the security configuration.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use Get-AccountSwitchKey (https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your configuration's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Identifies a security policy.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Ordered list of account protection rule IDs.
The version of the security configuration.
The name of the security policy. If not provided, the system generates a name automatically with the pattern `clone from <createFromSecurityPolicy>`.
